title: Promote keystone GatewaySpec into commonv1 for shared use
description:
# GitHub Issue #403: refactor(common): promote the keystone GatewaySpec into commonv1 so both operators share one type (Option B follow-up to #402)

## Context

When exposing the ControlPlane-projected Keystone through the shared Gateway
(#402), the c5c3 **api** package could not reuse the keystone operator's
`GatewaySpec` because the L1 api package intentionally has **no dependency** on
the keystone module (the DECISION recorded on `ServiceKeystoneSpec` in
`operators/c5c3/api/v1alpha1/controlplane_types.go`). #402 therefore landed
**Option A**: a curated, c5c3-local copy of the gateway shape
(`GatewaySpec` + `GatewayParentRefSpec`) that the L2 reconciler maps onto
`keystonev1alpha1.GatewaySpec`.

That curated copy is a deliberate, documented duplicate. This issue tracks the
**Option B** consolidation: promote the shared gateway shape into
`internal/common/types` (`commonv1`) so both operators reference **one** type and
the duplicate disappears.

## Why do this

- **Single source of truth.** Today `GatewaySpec`/`GatewayParentRefSpec` exist
  twice — `operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go` and (since #402)
  `operators/c5c3/api/v1alpha1/controlplane_types.go`. Field drift between the
  two (a new `path` default, an added annotation knob, validation markers) would
  be silent until the L2 mapping in `reconcile_keystone.go` breaks.
- **Mirrors the existing commonv1 discipline.** `DatabaseSpec`, `CacheSpec`,
  `ImageSpec`, `PolicySpec`, `SecretRefSpec` already live in `commonv1` and are
  shared by both the keystone and c5c3 CRDs for exactly this reason. `GatewaySpec`
  is the odd one out.
- **Scales to more services.** As `ServicesSpec` grows beyond Keystone, every
  service that wants external exposure would otherwise re-curate the same shape.

## Why it was deferred (and is its own issue)

It is heavier than the #402 quick-start goal warranted and touches **two**
operators' CRDs:

- Moving a type **out** of `keystonev1alpha1` is a breaking change for any
  external importer of `keystonev1alpha1.GatewaySpec` unless a type alias is left
  behind.
- It regenerates and re-syncs CRDs for **both** keystone and c5c3 (CRD drift
  across two operators — the `check-crd-drift` discipline).
- It needs `generate-common` (deepcopy for the new `commonv1` types) plus SPDX
  headers on any new file (the `check-spdx-reuse` discipline).

## Plan

### 1. Move the shape into `commonv1`
- [ ] Add `GatewaySpec` + `GatewayParentRefSpec` to `internal/common/types/types.go`
      (the canonical shape: `parentRef`, `hostname`, `path`, `annotations` /
      `name`, `namespace`, `sectionName`), carrying the `+kubebuilder` validation
      markers (`hostname` / `parentRef.name` MinLength=1).
- [ ] `make generate-common` to produce the `commonv1` deepcopy methods (SPDX
      header via the boilerplate).

### 2. Repoint the keystone operator
- [ ] Replace `keystonev1alpha1.GatewaySpec` / `GatewayParentRefSpec` with the
      `commonv1` types in `keystone_types.go` (`KeystoneSpec.Gateway` →
      `*commonv1.GatewaySpec`), the gateway reconciler / HTTPRoute builder, and the
      webhook validation.
- [ ] **Backward-compat decision:** leave a type alias
      `type GatewaySpec = commonv1.GatewaySpec` (and parentRef) in
      `keystonev1alpha1` to avoid breaking external importers, **or** do a clean
      move and document the break. Recommend the alias.

### 3. Repoint the c5c3 operator (remove the Option A duplicate)
- [ ] Delete the curated `GatewaySpec` / `GatewayParentRefSpec` from
      `operators/c5c3/api/v1alpha1/controlplane_types.go`; change
      `ServiceKeystoneSpec.Gateway` to `*commonv1.GatewaySpec`.
- [ ] Simplify the mapping in `reconcile_keystone.go`: with one shared type the
      reconciler can assign the gateway through (still distinct from
      `keystone.Spec.Gateway` only if keystone keeps its alias) — collapse the
      field-by-field copy.

### 4. Regenerate + sync (both operators)
- [ ] `make generate manifests sync-crds` and verify `make verify-crd-sync` —
      the c5c3 and keystone CRD `bases/` + helm `crds/` stay in lockstep. The CRD
      **schema** for the gateway block should be byte-stable; only the Go source of
      truth moves.

### 5. Docs + tests
- [ ] Point both CRD references (`docs/reference/c5c3/controlplane-crd.md`,
      `docs/reference/keystone/keystone-crd.md`) at the shared `commonv1.GatewaySpec`.
- [ ] Keystone gateway tests + c5c3 projection tests adjust to the shared type;
      behaviour must be unchanged.

## Acceptance criteria
- One `GatewaySpec` definition (in `commonv1`); no curated copy in the c5c3 api
  package; keystone either aliases or uses it directly.
- `make verify-crd-sync` green for both operators; gateway CRD schema unchanged.
- All existing keystone + c5c3 unit/envtest tests pass; the #402 external-exposure
  behaviour is preserved.

## Out of scope
- Any change to external-exposure *behaviour* — this is a pure type-consolidation
  refactor. The user-visible gateway/publicEndpoint semantics shipped in #402.

## Relationship
- Follow-up to #402 (which implemented Option A — the curated local copy). #402's
  "Design decision" section flagged this as the eventual consolidation.


**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/403

stories.title: commonv1 owns the single GatewaySpec definition
stories.want: GatewaySpec and GatewayParentRefSpec to live once in internal/common/types alongside DatabaseSpec/CacheSpec/PolicySpec, with deepcopy generated
stories.so_that: the gateway shape follows the same single-source-of-truth discipline as the other shared CRD shapes and cannot drift between operators
criteria: internal/common/types/types.go declares GatewaySpec and GatewayParentRefSpec with operator-neutral godoc
GatewaySpec.Hostname and GatewayParentRefSpec.Name carry +kubebuilder:validation:MinLength=1
make generate-common adds DeepCopy/DeepCopyInto for both types to internal/common/types/zz_generated.deepcopy.go

No new file is created (types added to existing types.go) so the existing SPDX header covers them; if a new file is added it carries SPDX-FileCopyrightText + SPDX-License-Identifier from hack/boilerplate.go.txt
go build ./... and go vet ./... pass for the internal/common module

stories.title: Keystone operator references the shared type without behaviour change
stories.want: keystonev1alpha1.GatewaySpec / GatewayParentRefSpec to become type aliases to the commonv1 types so KeystoneSpec.Gateway is *commonv1.GatewaySpec
stories.so_that: existing importers (in-repo and external) keep compiling while the duplicate definition is removed
criteria: keystone_types.go defines `type GatewaySpec = commonv1.GatewaySpec` and `type GatewayParentRefSpec = commonv1.GatewayParentRefSpec` (alias, not a new struct)
The KeystoneSpec.Gateway field godoc (CC-0065 description) is retained verbatim on the field declaration so the CRD `gateway` property description is unchanged
reconcile_httproute.go, reconcile_networkpolicy.go and keystone_webhook.go compile unchanged (all access keystone.Spec.Gateway.<field>)
make generate regenerates keystone zz_generated.deepcopy.go: standalone GatewaySpec/GatewayParentRefSpec DeepCopy funcs are removed and KeystoneSpec.DeepCopyInto references types.GatewaySpec
All existing keystone unit and envtest tests (keystone_webhook_test.go, integration_test.go, reconcile_httproute_test.go, reconcile_networkpolicy_test.go) pass unmodified

stories.title: c5c3 operator drops the Option A curated copy
stories.want: the curated GatewaySpec/GatewayParentRefSpec deleted from controlplane_types.go and ServiceKeystoneSpec.Gateway changed to *commonv1.GatewaySpec, with the L2 mapping collapsed
stories.so_that: the documented temporary duplicate disappears and the projection becomes a one-line assignment
criteria: controlplane_types.go no longer declares GatewaySpec or GatewayParentRefSpec; ServiceKeystoneSpec.Gateway is `*commonv1.GatewaySpec`
The ServiceKeystoneSpec.Gateway field godoc is retained so the ControlPlane CRD `gateway` property description is unchanged
reconcile_keystone.go replaces the field-by-field copy with a direct assignment (keystone.Spec.Gateway = gw.DeepCopy(); nil clears it)
The c5c3 L1 api package still imports ONLY commonv1 (no keystone import added); `go mod tidy` does not add operators/keystone to the api package's reachable imports
make generate regenerates c5c3 zz_generated.deepcopy.go: curated GatewaySpec/GatewayParentRefSpec DeepCopy funcs are removed and ServiceKeystoneSpec.DeepCopyInto references types.GatewaySpec

stories.title: CRD schema stays valid and in sync across both operators
stories.want: the gateway validation schema (types, minLength, required) to be byte-stable and both operators' bases/ + helm crds/ to stay in lockstep
stories.so_that: no Keystone or ControlPlane CR is rejected and clusters do not see schema regressions after the refactor
criteria: The structural gateway schema (minLength:1 on hostname & parentRef.name, required:[hostname,parentRef], required:[name]) is unchanged in both keystone.openstack.c5c3.io_keystones.yaml and c5c3.io_controlplanes.yaml
make manifests sync-crds regenerates and re-syncs both operators; make verify-crd-sync exits 0
The only CRD diff in the gateway block is the nested property descriptions (hostname/path/parentRef/annotations) converging to the canonical commonv1 godoc — the validation rules do not change
Existing keystone gateway e2e fixtures (tests/e2e/keystone/httproute, gateway-quick-start) remain schema-valid

stories.title: Documentation points at the shared commonv1 type
stories.want: both CRD reference docs to describe GatewaySpec as a shared commonv1 type rather than a curated/per-operator copy
stories.so_that: readers find one canonical gateway type description and cross-references resolve correctly
criteria: docs/reference/keystone/keystone-crd.md documents GatewaySpec/GatewayParentRefSpec under the existing "Shared Types (from internal/common/types)" section (or marks the existing GatewaySpec section as a shared commonv1 type)
docs/reference/c5c3/controlplane-crd.md GatewaySpec section is repointed at the shared commonv1.GatewaySpec instead of describing a curated local subset that mirrors keystone
All intra-doc anchor links (#gatewayspec, #gatewayparentrefspec) still resolve
Doc edits cite the feature ID CC-0111 where a change rationale is recorded

stories.title: External Go importer is not broken by the move
stories.want: keystonev1alpha1.GatewaySpec and keystonev1alpha1.GatewayParentRefSpec to remain referenceable symbols after the move
stories.so_that: code that imported the keystone gateway types continues to compile without edits
criteria: keystonev1alpha1.GatewaySpec and keystonev1alpha1.GatewayParentRefSpec still resolve (as aliases) and are assignable to/from commonv1 values
A value constructed as keystonev1alpha1.GatewaySpec{...} compiles and is identical-typed to commonv1.GatewaySpec{...}
No clean-break removal of the keystone symbols occurs unless the alias decision is explicitly reversed and the break documented

requirements.description: commonv1 SHALL define GatewaySpec and GatewayParentRefSpec as the single canonical gateway shape, carrying the existing validation markers, with controller-gen deepcopy generated.
requirements.rationale: Single source of truth; mirrors the existing commonv1 discipline for DatabaseSpec/CacheSpec/PolicySpec.

scenarios.name: Types added with markers
scenarios.when: GatewaySpec (parentRef, hostname, path, annotations) and GatewayParentRefSpec (name, namespace, sectionName) are added to internal/common/types/types.go
scenarios.then: Hostname and ParentRef.Name carry +kubebuilder:validation:MinLength=1 and the package compiles
and_then: godoc is operator-neutral (no Keystone-specific wording)
field tags match the existing keystone shape exactly (parentRef, hostname, path, annotations / name, namespace, sectionName)

scenarios.name: Deepcopy generated
scenarios.when: make generate-common runs
scenarios.then: internal/common/types/zz_generated.deepcopy.go gains DeepCopy/DeepCopyInto for GatewaySpec and GatewayParentRefSpec
and_then: the generated file keeps its SPDX header and DO NOT EDIT banner
the Annotations map is deep-copied (allocated, not aliased)

scenarios.name: No SPDX regression
scenarios.when: check-spdx-reuse is run after the change
scenarios.then: no new uncovered file is reported
and_then: types are added to the existing types.go so no new header is needed; any new file added instead carries the boilerplate SPDX header

requirements.description: The keystone operator SHALL reference the commonv1 gateway types via type aliases, preserving all current reconciler/webhook behaviour and external-symbol compatibility.
requirements.rationale: Removes the keystone-local definition while keeping importers compiling and behaviour identical (#402 external exposure preserved).

scenarios.name: Alias replaces local struct
scenarios.when: the keystone-local GatewaySpec/GatewayParentRefSpec struct definitions are replaced by `type GatewaySpec = commonv1.GatewaySpec` and `type GatewayParentRefSpec = commonv1.GatewayParentRefSpec`
scenarios.then: KeystoneSpec.Gateway resolves to *commonv1.GatewaySpec and the package builds
and_then: the KeystoneSpec.Gateway field godoc (CC-0065) is retained on the field so the CRD property description is unchanged

scenarios.name: Consumers unchanged
scenarios.when: the keystone module is rebuilt
scenarios.then: reconcile_httproute.go, reconcile_networkpolicy.go, and keystone_webhook.go compile without edits
and_then: existing keystone gateway unit + envtest tests pass unmodified
keystonev1alpha1.GatewaySpec{...} literals in tests still compile

scenarios.name: Deepcopy regenerated
scenarios.when: make generate runs for keystone
scenarios.then: standalone GatewaySpec/GatewayParentRefSpec DeepCopy funcs are removed from keystone zz_generated.deepcopy.go and KeystoneSpec.DeepCopyInto allocates types.GatewaySpec
and_then: go vet passes
the regenerated file is committed

requirements.description: The c5c3 operator SHALL delete the Option A curated GatewaySpec/GatewayParentRefSpec, change ServiceKeystoneSpec.Gateway to *commonv1.GatewaySpec, and collapse the L2 projection mapping.
requirements.rationale: Removes the documented temporary duplicate; with one shared type the field-by-field copy is unnecessary.

scenarios.name: Curated copy removed
scenarios.when: controlplane_types.go is edited
scenarios.then: GatewaySpec and GatewayParentRefSpec are no longer declared in the c5c3 api package and ServiceKeystoneSpec.Gateway is *commonv1.GatewaySpec
and_then: the ServiceKeystoneSpec.Gateway field godoc is retained so the ControlPlane CRD property description is unchanged
the Option A DECISION comment block is removed or updated to record the CC-0111 consolidation

scenarios.name: Mapping collapsed
scenarios.when: reconcileKeystone projects a non-nil gateway
scenarios.then: it assigns keystone.Spec.Gateway = cp.Spec.Services.Keystone.Gateway.DeepCopy() (and sets it to nil when the source is nil) instead of copying each field
and_then: keystonePublicEndpoint still derives https://{hostname}/v3 when publicEndpoint is empty and gateway is set
TestReconcileKeystone_GatewayProjection still asserts the projected keystone gateway fields equal the source

scenarios.name: L1 dependency invariant preserved
scenarios.when: go mod tidy is run in the c5c3 module after the change
scenarios.then: the c5c3 api (L1) package still imports only commonv1 / apimachinery / controller-runtime — no operators/keystone import is introduced at L1
and_then: the keystone require+replace in c5c3 go.mod remains (used by the L2 controller), but nothing new is added by this refactor

requirements.description: The gateway CRD validation schema SHALL remain byte-stable for both operators, and both bases/ + helm crds/ SHALL stay in sync.
requirements.rationale: Acceptance criterion: gateway schema unchanged; only the Go source of truth moves. check-crd-drift discipline across two operators.

scenarios.name: Validation rules unchanged
scenarios.when: make manifests regenerates both CRDs
scenarios.then: the gateway block keeps minLength:1 on hostname and parentRef.name, required:[hostname,parentRef], and required:[name] under parentRef in BOTH keystone and c5c3 CRDs
and_then: the gateway property types (object/string/map) are unchanged

scenarios.name: Descriptions converge (expected, non-functional)
scenarios.when: the regenerated CRDs are diffed against the committed versions
scenarios.then: the only gateway-block change is the nested property descriptions (hostname/path/parentRef/annotations) converging to the canonical commonv1 godoc; the top-level `gateway` property description (from the field godoc) is unchanged in each operator
and_then: the diff is reviewed and accepted as a description-only, non-validation change

scenarios.name: Helm sync green
scenarios.when: make sync-crds then make verify-crd-sync runs
scenarios.then: verify-crd-sync exits 0 for both operators
and_then: the helm crds/ copies match bases/ ignoring the cross-reference comment header

requirements.description: The shared commonv1 GatewaySpec SHALL have a deepcopy unit test proving independent (non-aliased) copies, mirroring the existing DatabaseTLSSpec test.
requirements.rationale: TEST_COVERAGE: new shared type must have accompanying tests; the deepcopy of the Annotations map is the only non-trivial copy behaviour.

scenarios.name: Populated value deep-copies independently
scenarios.when: a populated *commonv1.GatewaySpec (with a non-empty Annotations map) is DeepCopy()'d
scenarios.then: the clone is a new pointer, equal by value, and mutating clone.Annotations does not affect the original
and_then: mutating clone.ParentRef does not affect the original

scenarios.name: Nil deep-copies to nil
scenarios.when: a nil *commonv1.GatewaySpec is DeepCopy()'d
scenarios.then: the result is nil
and_then: a GatewaySpec with nil Annotations deep-copies to a value whose Annotations is nil

scenarios.name: Field-type assertion
scenarios.when: the c5c3 controlplane_types_test compile-time type assertion runs
scenarios.then: assigning &commonv1.GatewaySpec{} to spec.Services.Keystone.Gateway compiles (proving the field is exactly the shared type, not a local copy)
and_then: the test fails to compile if a local GatewaySpec is reintroduced

requirements.description: All existing keystone and c5c3 unit/envtest tests SHALL pass after the refactor and the #402 external-exposure behaviour SHALL be preserved.
requirements.rationale: Acceptance criterion: behaviour unchanged; this is a pure type-consolidation refactor.

scenarios.name: c5c3 tests repointed and green
scenarios.when: reconcile_keystone_test.go and reconcile_korc_test.go (which use c5c3v1alpha1.GatewaySpec / GatewayParentRefSpec) are repointed to commonv1
scenarios.then: go test ./... passes for the c5c3 module
and_then: TestReconcileKeystone_GatewayProjection, TestReconcileKeystone_GatewayNilStaysInCluster, TestReconcileKeystone_PublicEndpointDerivedFromHostname still pass with identical assertions

scenarios.name: keystone tests green unmodified
scenarios.when: go test ./... runs for the keystone module
scenarios.then: all gateway webhook/controller/envtest tests pass without source edits
and_then: the HTTPRoute built from spec.gateway is byte-identical to pre-refactor

scenarios.name: Behaviour preserved end-to-end
scenarios.when: a ControlPlane with services.keystone.gateway set is reconciled (fake client / envtest)
scenarios.then: the projected Keystone CR carries the same gateway fields and the same derived publicEndpoint as before the refactor
and_then: a nil gateway still clears keystone.Spec.Gateway and advertises no public endpoint

requirements.description: Both CRD reference docs SHALL point at the shared commonv1.GatewaySpec instead of describing per-operator copies.
requirements.rationale: Docs drift discipline; the docs currently describe a keystone-local type and a c5c3 "curated local subset".

scenarios.name: keystone doc updated
scenarios.when: docs/reference/keystone/keystone-crd.md is edited
scenarios.then: GatewaySpec/GatewayParentRefSpec are presented as shared commonv1 types (placed in or linked from the existing "Shared Types (from internal/common/types)" section)
and_then: the existing #gatewayspec / #gatewayparentrefspec anchors still resolve so c5c3 cross-links do not break

scenarios.name: c5c3 doc updated
scenarios.when: docs/reference/c5c3/controlplane-crd.md is edited
scenarios.then: the GatewaySpec section no longer calls it a curated local subset that mirrors keystone; it links to the shared commonv1.GatewaySpec
and_then: the `gateway` field row in the ServiceKeystoneSpec table links to the shared type

scenarios.name: Feature ID cited
scenarios.when: a rationale line is added to either doc
scenarios.then: it references CC-0111 (the consolidation follow-up to CC-0110)

requirements.description: The Go workspace dependency lockstep and REUSE/SPDX state SHALL be preserved; the refactor introduces no version drift and no uncovered files.
requirements.rationale: check-go-workspace-deps and check-spdx-reuse disciplines; controller-runtime/k8s pins must stay aligned across modules.

scenarios.name: No dep drift
scenarios.when: check-go-workspace-deps runs after the change
scenarios.then: controller-runtime, k8s.io/api, k8s.io/apimachinery, k8s.io/client-go, the Go directive and toolchain remain identical across internal/common and both operators
and_then: no go.mod require is added or bumped by this refactor

scenarios.name: SPDX clean
scenarios.when: check-spdx-reuse runs
scenarios.then: every changed/added .go file (including regenerated zz_generated files) carries matching SPDX headers
and_then: generated deepcopy files keep the boilerplate header

requirements.description: The fixture set SHALL remain valid against the regenerated CRDs (no fixture references a removed field; gateway fixtures still validate).
requirements.rationale: check-fixture-drift discipline; the gateway structural schema is byte-stable so fixtures should remain valid, but this must be confirmed.

scenarios.name: keystone gateway fixtures valid
scenarios.when: check-fixture-drift (or kubectl --dry-run against the regenerated CRD) is run on tests/e2e/keystone/httproute and gateway-quick-start fixtures
scenarios.then: every fixture still validates against the regenerated keystone CRD
and_then: no gateway sub-field used in a fixture was removed or renamed

scenarios.name: No c5c3 controlplane gateway fixture regression
scenarios.when: the c5c3 CRD is regenerated
scenarios.then: any ControlPlane fixture (if present) that sets services.keystone.gateway still validates

tasks.title: Add GatewaySpec + GatewayParentRefSpec to internal/common/types/types.go (REQ-001). Copy the canonical shape (parentRef, hostname, path, annotations / name, namespace, sectionName) with operator-neutral godoc and the +kubebuilder:validation:MinLength=1 markers on Hostname and ParentRef.Name. Add to the existing file (no new file) so the SPDX header already covers it.

Run `make generate-common` and verify GatewaySpec/GatewayParentRefSpec DeepCopy funcs land in internal/common/types/zz_generated.deepcopy.go (REQ-001). Add TestGatewaySpec_DeepCopy + nil-Annotations case to internal/common/types/types_test.go mirroring TestDatabaseTLSSpec_DeepCopy.

In operators/keystone/api/v1alpha1/keystone_types.go replace the GatewaySpec/GatewayParentRefSpec struct definitions with `type GatewaySpec = commonv1.GatewaySpec` and `type GatewayParentRefSpec = commonv1.GatewayParentRefSpec` (REQ-002). Keep the KeystoneSpec.Gateway field godoc (CC-0065) verbatim on the field so the CRD property description is unchanged.

Run `make generate` for keystone; confirm keystone zz_generated.deepcopy.go drops the standalone GatewaySpec/GatewayParentRefSpec DeepCopy funcs and KeystoneSpec.DeepCopyInto references types.GatewaySpec (REQ-002). Build the keystone module and run its existing gateway unit/envtest tests unmodified to confirm reconcile_httproute.go, reconcile_networkpolicy.go and keystone_webhook.go are unaffected.

In operators/c5c3/api/v1alpha1/controlplane_types.go delete the curated GatewaySpec/GatewayParentRefSpec definitions and change ServiceKeystoneSpec.Gateway to `*commonv1.GatewaySpec` (REQ-003). Keep the field godoc; replace the Option A DECISION comment with a one-line CC-0111 note recording the consolidation.

Collapse the L2 mapping in operators/c5c3/internal/controller/reconcile_keystone.go (REQ-003): replace the field-by-field copy with `keystone.Spec.Gateway = cp.Spec.Services.Keystone.Gateway.DeepCopy()` (nil clears it). Leave a `# DECISION:` style comment justifying DeepCopy over direct pointer share. keystonePublicEndpoint is unchanged.

Repoint c5c3 tests to commonv1 (REQ-003, REQ-006): in reconcile_keystone_test.go and reconcile_korc_test.go swap c5c3v1alpha1.GatewaySpec/GatewayParentRefSpec for commonv1 equivalents; in controlplane_types_test.go extend TestControlPlaneSpecReusesCommonTypes with the gateway compile-time type assertion. Update any 'curated' wording in test comments to 'shared'.

Run `make generate` for c5c3; confirm c5c3 zz_generated.deepcopy.go drops the curated GatewaySpec/GatewayParentRefSpec DeepCopy funcs and ServiceKeystoneSpec.DeepCopyInto references types.GatewaySpec (REQ-003). Build the c5c3 module and run its unit tests.

Run `go mod tidy` in the c5c3 module and confirm the L1 api package (operators/c5c3/api/...) still imports only commonv1/apimachinery/controller-runtime — no operators/keystone import introduced at L1 (REQ-003, REQ-008). Confirm no go.mod require/version changes (check-go-workspace-deps stays green).

Run `make manifests sync-crds` then `make verify-crd-sync` for both operators (REQ-004). Review the gateway-block diff in both bases/ + helm crds/: assert the validation schema (minLength:1, required arrays, types) is byte-stable and the only change is nested property descriptions converging to the commonv1 godoc. Commit the regenerated + synced CRDs.

Validate fixtures against the regenerated CRDs (REQ-009): run the existing invalid-cr / e2e gateway fixtures through check-fixture-drift (or kubectl apply --dry-run=server with the regenerated keystone CRD) to confirm tests/e2e/keystone/httproute and gateway-quick-start fixtures still validate.

Update docs/reference/keystone/keystone-crd.md (REQ-007): present GatewaySpec/GatewayParentRefSpec as shared commonv1 types (fold into or link from the existing 'Shared Types (from internal/common/types)' section), keeping the #gatewayspec / #gatewayparentrefspec anchors intact so c5c3 cross-links resolve. Cite CC-0111.

Update docs/reference/c5c3/controlplane-crd.md (REQ-007): repoint the GatewaySpec section and the ServiceKeystoneSpec.gateway field row at the shared commonv1.GatewaySpec; remove the 'curated local subset that mirrors keystone field-for-field' framing. Cite CC-0111.

Full verification sweep (REQ-006, REQ-008): run go test ./... for internal/common, keystone, and c5c3 modules; run check-spdx-reuse, check-crd-drift, check-doc-drift, and check-go-workspace-deps. Confirm all green and that the #402 external-exposure behaviour is preserved end-to-end.

test_specifications.story: commonv1 owns the single GatewaySpec definition
test_specifications.expected: [unit] DeepCopy of a populated *GatewaySpec returns a new, value-equal pointer; mutating clone.Annotations / clone.ParentRef does not affect the original; DeepCopy of a nil *GatewaySpec returns nil. Mirrors TestDatabaseTLSSpec_DeepCopy.

test_specifications.story: commonv1 owns the single GatewaySpec definition
test_specifications.expected: [unit] DeepCopy of a GatewaySpec whose Annotations is nil produces a clone whose Annotations is nil (no spurious empty-map allocation).

test_specifications.story: Keystone operator references the shared type without behaviour change
test_specifications.expected: [unit] These pre-existing tests, which construct GatewaySpec{}/GatewayParentRefSpec{} literals in-package, MUST continue to compile and pass unmodified — proving the type alias is transparent to in-package literals.

test_specifications.story: Keystone operator references the shared type without behaviour change
test_specifications.expected: [unit/integration] The HTTPRoute built from spec.gateway (parentRef, hostname, path default "/", annotations passthrough) is unchanged after the alias swap; all existing assertions pass without edits.

test_specifications.story: CRD schema stays valid and in sync across both operators
test_specifications.expected: [integration] envtest applies a Keystone CR with spec.gateway against the regenerated CRD and the HTTPRoute reconcile + status.endpoint derivation behave identically to pre-refactor.

test_specifications.story: c5c3 operator drops the Option A curated copy
test_specifications.expected: [unit] Add a compile-time assertion `spec.Services.Keystone.Gateway = &commonv1.GatewaySpec{ParentRef: commonv1.GatewayParentRefSpec{Name: "gw"}, Hostname: "h"}` — fails to compile if a local GatewaySpec is reintroduced.

test_specifications.story: c5c3 operator drops the Option A curated copy

review_criteria: BLOCKER: Exactly one GatewaySpec/GatewayParentRefSpec definition exists — in commonv1; the c5c3 api package has no local copy and keystone only aliases (verified by grep for `type GatewaySpec struct` returning a single hit under internal/common/types).

BLOCKER: make verify-crd-sync exits 0 for BOTH operators; the gateway-block validation schema (minLength:1 on hostname & parentRef.name, required:[hostname,parentRef], required:[name]) is unchanged in both CRDs — only nested descriptions converge.
BLOCKER: All pre-existing keystone gateway tests pass UNMODIFIED (alias transparency) and the repointed c5c3 gateway tests pass with identical assertions (behaviour preserved).
CRITICAL: The c5c3 L1 api package still imports only commonv1/apimachinery/controller-runtime after `go mod tidy` — the L1-no-keystone-dependency invariant from CC-0110 is intact.
CRITICAL: No go.mod require/version changes; check-go-workspace-deps confirms controller-runtime/k8s pins stay aligned across all three modules.
CRITICAL: commonv1.GatewaySpec has a deepcopy unit test proving the Annotations map and ParentRef are copied independently and nil → nil.
CRITICAL: check-spdx-reuse is clean — no new uncovered file (types added to existing types.go); regenerated zz_generated files keep their SPDX headers.
MINOR: Both CRD reference docs describe GatewaySpec as a shared commonv1 type with working anchors and cite CC-0111; no stale 'curated local subset' wording remains.
MINOR: The reconcile_keystone.go mapping is reduced to a single DeepCopy assignment with a DECISION comment, and 'curated' wording in c5c3 comments/tests is updated to 'shared'.
MINOR: golangci-lint and gofumpt (repo-pinned versions) are clean on all changed Go files.
implementation_notes:
PURE TYPE-CONSOLIDATION REFACTOR (CC-0111, GitHub #403) — Option B follow-up to CC-0110/#402. No user-visible behaviour change.

ARCHITECTURE / DATA FLOW (3 modules, ASCII):

  internal/common/types (commonv1)  <-- single source of truth
        ^                 ^
        | alias           | *commonv1.GatewaySpec (field)
  keystone/api/v1alpha1   c5c3/api/v1alpha1
  GatewaySpec=commonv1.*  ServiceKeystoneSpec.Gateway *commonv1.GatewaySpec
        ^                       |
        | *commonv1.GatewaySpec | DeepCopy()
  KeystoneSpec.Gateway  <-------+  (L2 reconcile_keystone.go projects cp.gateway -> keystone.gateway)

Data flow at reconcile: ControlPlane.Spec.Services.Keystone.Gateway (*commonv1.GatewaySpec) -> reconcileKeystone DeepCopy -> Keystone.Spec.Gateway (*commonv1.GatewaySpec, via keystone alias) -> keystone-operator buildKeystoneHTTPRoute -> HTTPRoute. With one shared type the field-by-field copy collapses to one DeepCopy assignment.

KEY DECISION 1 (alias for keystone, per #403 recommendation): use `type GatewaySpec = commonv1.GatewaySpec` (and parentRef). An alias is a transparent compile-time identity — every in-package literal (keystone_webhook_test.go uses GatewaySpec{}), every qualified reference (keystonev1alpha1.GatewaySpec in keystone controller tests and historically in c5c3), and every field access (keystone.Spec.Gateway.Hostname in reconcile_httproute.go / reconcile_networkpolicy.go / keystone_webhook.go) keeps compiling with ZERO edits. This is why keystone needs no test or reconciler changes. The alias also preserves backward-compat for any external importer.

KEY DECISION 2 (clean delete for c5c3): the c5c3 curated copy was always a documented temporary duplicate (Option A). Delete it outright; the only in-repo consumers are c5c3's own reconciler + tests, which we update to commonv1. Do NOT add a keystone import to the c5c3 L1 api package — that invariant (CC-0110 plan decision #3) is the whole reason the curated copy existed.

KEY DECISION 3 (CRD descriptions WILL change, schema will NOT): controller-gen reads +kubebuilder markers from imported packages (proven: commonv1.DatabaseTLSSpec.Mode enum already renders into the keystone CRD), so the MinLength markers render identically once moved. BUT the nested gateway property DESCRIPTIONS (hostname/path/parentRef/annotations) currently differ between keystone and c5c3 godocs; after both reference commonv1 they CONVERGE to the single canonical commonv1 godoc. So expect a description-only diff in BOTH CRD bases + helm crds/. The top-level `gateway` property description comes from the FIELD godoc (KeystoneSpec.Gateway / ServiceKeystoneSpec.Gateway), which stays put — keep those field godocs. The acceptance criterion 'gateway CRD schema unchanged' means the VALIDATION schema (types/minLength/required), which is byte-stable; descriptions are non-functional. Write the canonical commonv1 godoc operator-neutrally (no 'Keystone API' / 'projected Keystone' wording) so it reads correctly in both CRDs.

CANONICAL commonv1 GODOC (suggested, operator-neutral): GatewaySpec — 'configures the Gateway API HTTPRoute used to expose a service externally. The operator plays the application-developer role: it manages only the HTTPRoute; the referenced Gateway (and GatewayClass) are platform-team infrastructure provisioned outside the operator.' Fields: ParentRef ('identifies the Gateway the HTTPRoute attaches to'), Hostname ('externally reachable host (SNI / Host header) matched by the HTTPRoute; required', MinLength=1), Path ('URL path prefix matched by the HTTPRoute; the reconciler defaults it to "/" when empty'), Annotations ('passed through to the HTTPRoute metadata verbatim for implementation-specific config (rate limits, timeouts, CORS)'). GatewayParentRefSpec: Name (required, MinLength=1), Namespace (optional; empty = the consuming CR's namespace; cross-namespace needs a ReferenceGrant), SectionName (optional listener target).

GENERATION ORDER (Makefile, OPERATORS='keystone c5c3'): make generate-common (commonv1 deepcopy) -> make generate (both operators' deepcopy) -> make manifests (both CRDs + webhook) -> make sync-crds (copy bases/ -> helm crds/ with header) -> make verify-crd-sync (diff ignoring ^#). Run from repo root.

PITFALLS: (1) Don't create a new file in commonv1 for these types unless you add the SPDX boilerplate header — adding to types.go is simpler and header-free. (2) After the keystone alias, DON'T hand-edit zz_generated.deepcopy.go — let make generate remove the now-duplicate funcs. (3) Don't share the Annotations map by pointer-assigning cp.gateway directly without DeepCopy in the mutate closure — DeepCopy keeps the child object's map independent of the parent CR. (4) Remember c5c3 has THREE CRDs in bases/ (controlplanes, credentialrotations, secretaggregates) — only controlplanes changes; verify-crd-sync covers all. (5) CI already runs verify-crd-sync / spdx / drift checks — no new GitHub Actions workflow is needed (no new test tier is introduced); do not add a CI task.

TEST TIERS PRESENT: unit (_test.go alongside packages, incl. commonv1 types_test.go), integration (envtest, keystone integration_test.go), e2e (Chainsaw under tests/e2e/keystone). This refactor adds one unit test (commonv1 deepcopy) + one compile-time assertion (c5c3), repoints c5c3 unit tests, and relies on the unchanged keystone unit/integration + e2e fixtures to prove behaviour is preserved.
